# Questions in Livestream 138

Live Stream #138: St. Nick is Watching You

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gULL91393YI?t=432" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">7:12</a> | Has answering a question in a Q&A ever made you change your mind? | Yes. He says a question about the Magi led him to rethink older assumptions about Persian or Zoroastrian astrologers. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gULL91393YI?t=1337" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">22:17</a> | Do Amarna letters EA 227 and EA 228 mentioning Hazor and its ruler praising the Egyptian king rule out an early date for Hazor's destruction? | Yes. He says the Amarna evidence shows Hazor still active and aggressive, which does not fit an early destruction. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gULL91393YI?t=1548" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">25:48</a> | Is low Christology in biblical academia scholarly, or is it biased toward minimalist liberal views? | He says definitions matter, but low Christology often begins with Jesus's earthly ministry rather than incarnation, and scholarly conclusions can be shaped by prior commitments. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gULL91393YI?t=1810" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">30:10</a> | Is Grace School of Theology in The Woodlands, Texas a legitimate school? | Yes. He says it is accredited and evangelical, though small and unusually open in admissions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gULL91393YI?t=1979" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">32:59</a> | What should ancient Egypt have done to keep its power? | He says Egypt needed a regular army instead of relying on volunteers and later Libyan mercenaries who gained too much power. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gULL91393YI?t=2140" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">35:40</a> | What biblical publications or organizations produce good articles and research? | He names ETS for conservative scholarship and points to specialized groups such as ASOR for ancient Near Eastern work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gULL91393YI?t=2308" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">38:28</a> | In Isaiah 53, is the servant Israel, Jesus, or Israel as an archetype of Jesus? | He argues the passage is not simply about Israel and reads the suffering servant as ultimately pointing to Christ. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gULL91393YI?t=3135" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">52:15</a> | What is your stance on Israel belonging to the Jewish people? | He says modern claimants all have complicated claims, but Israel probably has the strongest claim in his view. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gULL91393YI?t=3291" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">54:51</a> | Is the New Testament's silence about the temple destruction strong evidence that the books predate AD 70? | No. He calls that an argument from silence or negative evidence, though he still thinks some New Testament texts predate AD 70. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gULL91393YI?t=3510" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">58:30</a> | Does 1 Corinthians 6:16 mean that those who have sex are married in God's eyes? | He says the passage speaks of a real one-flesh union, but that does not make every sexual union a full covenant marriage. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gULL91393YI?t=3968" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:06:08</a> | What do you think of theories that the pyramids were nuclear fission plants? | He laughs off the proposal as extremely unlikely and compares it to other implausible pyramid theories. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gULL91393YI?t=4327" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:12:07</a> | Do pyramids block cell phone signals inside all that rock? | Yes. He says the rock and remote locations make signals poor, and some areas are also affected by nearby military facilities. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gULL91393YI?t=4476" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:14:36</a> | What exactly is the Gunung Padang or "boat pyramid" controversy? | He says he is not familiar enough with the controversy and would need to look it up. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gULL91393YI?t=5017" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:23:37</a> | In Daniel 10:18, does Gabriel admit to causing the Greco-Persian War? | He treats the passage as spiritual conflict involving Persia and Greece, but not as a simple admission that Gabriel caused the war. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gULL91393YI?t=5310" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:28:30</a> | What are your thoughts on Gospel authorship? | He generally maintains traditional authorship and says the early church's early consensus deserves serious weight. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gULL91393YI?t=5562" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:32:42</a> | Is lust acceptable within marriage, and can Matthew 5:28 refer to a wife? | He says the Greek assumes desiring a woman not already one's wife, so the passage concerns adulterous desire rather than marital attraction. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gULL91393YI?t=6182" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:43:02</a> | Is the Sudarium of Oviedo a misunderstood artifact? | He says he had to identify the artifact first and treats it alongside claimed burial cloths such as the Shroud of Turin. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gULL91393YI?t=6817" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:53:37</a> | When were the Hebrew Scriptures compiled, edited, or distributed, and does that affect interpretation? | He says the books were compiled gradually, often through temple or scribal processes, and each book must be considered on its own terms. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gULL91393YI?t=7054" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:57:34</a> | Could Mary have been a source for Luke? | Yes. He thinks Mary was very likely one of Luke's sources. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gULL91393YI?t=7225" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:00:25</a> | Do you believe the flood was global or regional? | He says he has no firm opinion and thinks the point of the text is ritual and theological rather than deciding that scientific question. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gULL91393YI?t=7464" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:04:24</a> | Are there conservative-leaning moderate scholars you recommend instead of apologetics-focused scholars? | He says most conservative scholars he knows are not apologetics-focused, and the bigger problem is that many do not communicate well to lay audiences. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gULL91393YI?t=8644" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:24:04</a> | Could Mary have had daughters but no other sons, explaining Jesus's sisters and John replacing him at the cross? | He says the masculine plural language generally includes brothers and sisters, so the text does not support only daughters. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gULL91393YI?t=8832" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:27:12</a> | What is your stance on what the Nephilim are? | He reads the Nephilim as cursed offspring of a priestly caste that broke sacred vows. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gULL91393YI?t=9921" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:45:21</a> | What are good resources for tracking down lesser-known Church Fathers? | He recommends Migne's volumes and the Christian Classics Ethereal Library, while noting Migne is broad rather than high-quality editing. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gULL91393YI?t=10422" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:53:42</a> | Would you consider making structured courses for churches, such as archaeology of the Exodus with videos and a workbook? | He says maybe, but producing a course would take major time, funding, and sponsorship. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gULL91393YI?t=11266" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:07:46</a> | Why are the Gospel accounts so randomly different? | He points to didactic arrangement and says the Gospel writers arrange material for teaching rather than as identical chronological transcripts. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gULL91393YI?t=11756" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:15:56</a> | Did ancient Egyptians have anything like modern museums? | No. He says modern museums are a much later phenomenon, though older elites had curiosity cabinets and collections. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gULL91393YI?t=11907" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:18:27</a> | What is your response to people who argue that Yahweh originated as a foreign deity in a pantheon? | He says Yahweh's origins are still debated and mentions the Kenite hypothesis as plausible, while rejecting simplistic pantheon claims. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gULL91393YI?t=12237" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:23:57</a> | Are you a full preterist or a partial preterist? | Neither. He says he is a futurist; full preterism is heretical, while partial preterism is within orthodoxy but not his view. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gULL91393YI?t=12722" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:32:02</a> | If preterism were true, what earth are we living in now? | He says full preterism would imply Christ's return already happened, which conflicts with the creeds and leaves this world as the supposed kingdom. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gULL91393YI?t=12912" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:35:12</a> | What do you think of the Septuagint reading of Isaiah 9:10 about building a tower? | He reads it in context and treats the Septuagint wording as an interesting textual issue, not as a settled doctrinal point. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gULL91393YI?t=13486" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:44:46</a> | Did the Library of Alexandria have humidity issues ruining papyrus? | Yes. He says humid libraries had to recopy texts repeatedly because papyri could mold, mildew, and rot. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gULL91393YI?t=14442" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:00:42</a> | Why is the teaching of Aramaic not more widespread? | He says there is less demand and less biblical Aramaic than Greek or Hebrew, so fewer courses and instructors exist. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gULL91393YI?t=14829" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:07:09</a> | Why not start the livestream an hour earlier and end an hour earlier? | He says they may consider it now that the channel is full time, but the risk is simply extending the stream instead of ending earlier. |
